name: github
title: GitHub
description: Repository info panels on entities with a repoUrl, GitHub Actions workflow dispatch, and optional GitHub OAuth SSO.
longDescription:
The GitHub plugin connects Gantry to your GitHub organization, enriching service entities with live repository data — open pull requests, recent commits, workflow runs, and contributor stats. It supports both Personal Access Tokens and GitHub App authentication for org-wide access. Optionally, enable GitHub OAuth SSO so your team can sign in to Gantry with their GitHub identities instead of a local password.

Once installed, any entity with a `spec.repoUrl` pointing to GitHub will automatically show a live GitHub tab with real-time repo info.
features: Live repository panel on Service, API, and Infrastructure entities
View open PRs, recent commits, topics, stars, and forks
Trigger GitHub Actions workflows directly from Gantry Actions
GitHub App or Personal Access Token authentication
GitHub OAuth SSO — sign in to Gantry with your GitHub account
Auto-discovers org repositories and syncs them as Service entities
author: Gantry

authMode.title: Authentication Mode
authMode.description: How Gantry authenticates with the GitHub API.

personalAccessToken.title: Personal Access Token
personalAccessToken.description: GitHub PAT with repo and read:org scopes. Required when authMode=pat.

appId.title: GitHub App ID
appId.description: Numeric App ID from your GitHub App settings. Required when authMode=app.

privateKey.title: Private Key (PEM)
privateKey.description: PEM-encoded RSA private key for your GitHub App. Required when authMode=app.

installationId.title: Installation ID
installationId.description: GitHub App installation ID for your organization. Required when authMode=app.

ssoEnabled.title: Enable GitHub SSO
ssoEnabled.description: Allow users to sign in to Gantry with their GitHub account via OAuth.

oauthClientId.title: OAuth App Client ID
oauthClientId.description: Client ID from your GitHub OAuth App. Required when ssoEnabled=true.

oauthClientSecret.title: OAuth App Client Secret
oauthClientSecret.description: Client secret from your GitHub OAuth App. Required when ssoEnabled=true.

defaultRole.title: Default SSO User Role
defaultRole.description: Role assigned to new users who sign in via GitHub SSO.

name: pagerduty
title: PagerDuty
description: On-call schedule panels for Services and Teams, plus incident management widgets.
longDescription:
The PagerDuty plugin adds on-call schedule and incident panels to Service and Team entity pages. Link your Gantry entities to PagerDuty services via a simple annotation and your engineers will see who is currently on-call, the active escalation policy, and a live list of open incidents — all without leaving the service catalog.

Set the `pagerduty.com/service-id` annotation on any Service or Team entity to activate the panel automatically.
features: On-call schedule panel on Service and Team entities
Real-time open incident list with severity and status
Escalation policy overview
Annotation-driven setup — no code required
Configurable annotation key for PagerDuty service ID mapping
author: Gantry

serviceIdAnnotation.title: Service ID Annotation
serviceIdAnnotation.description: Entity annotation key that holds the PagerDuty service ID

name: kubernetes
title: Kubernetes
description: Auto-discovers services, deployments, and namespaces from your clusters and syncs them to the catalog. Adds resource status panels.
longDescription:
The Kubernetes plugin connects to one or more clusters and continuously discovers your workloads. Namespaces become Environment entities, Deployments become Service entities, and Kubernetes Services become Infrastructure entities — automatically populating your catalog without any manual YAML.

A live workload panel on service pages shows pod health, restart counts, resource requests/limits, and a streaming container log viewer. Configure multiple clusters, per-cluster namespace filters, and a global label selector to scope exactly which resources are imported.
features: Auto-discovery of Namespaces, Deployments, and Services into the catalog
Multi-cluster support with independent per-cluster namespace filters
Live pod status, restart counts, and resource usage on entity pages
Streaming container log viewer in the entity panel
Label selector for scoping which resources to import
Configurable periodic background sync (default: every 5 minutes)
author: Gantry

clusters.title: Clusters
clusters.description: One or more Kubernetes clusters to discover resources from.

name.title: Cluster Name
name.description: Display name for this cluster (e.g. prod-us-east)

caData.title: CA Certificate
caData.description: Base64-encoded cluster CA certificate. Leave empty to skip TLS verification.

namespaces.title: Namespaces
namespaces.description: Comma-separated list of namespaces to watch. Leave empty to discover all namespaces.

labelSelector.title: Label Selector
labelSelector.description: Filter discovered resources by label (e.g. app.kubernetes.io/managed-by=gantry). Applied to all clusters.

syncInterval.title: Sync Interval
syncInterval.description: How often to automatically sync (e.g. 5m, 1h). Leave empty to sync manually only.

name: slack
title: Slack
description: Send notifications on action runs, approvals, and sync errors to Slack channels.
longDescription:
The Slack plugin lets Gantry post structured notifications to your Slack workspace. When an Action run completes, an approval is requested, or a catalog sync fails, Gantry sends a rich message to the configured Slack channel — keeping your team informed without having to check the UI.

Messages include direct links back to the relevant entity or action run so engineers can jump straight to the right context.
features: Action run completion notifications with pass/fail status
Approval-request messages with direct deep-links into Gantry
Catalog sync error alerts with error details
Configurable default channel with per-action overrides
Rich message formatting with entity context
author: Gantry

name: datadog
title: Datadog
description: Embed SLO status, error rates, and service metrics panels on Service entities.
longDescription:
The Datadog plugin embeds live SLO status, error-rate trends, and service metrics directly into Gantry entity pages. Engineers see at a glance whether a service is meeting its SLOs and can review recent error rates without switching tools.

Use entity annotations to map Gantry services to their Datadog service names or SLO IDs. Supports all Datadog sites including US, EU, US-Gov, and AP1.
features: SLO status badge and remaining error budget on Service entities
Error rate and latency trend charts
Service metrics overview pulled from Datadog APM
Annotation-driven mapping — no code required
Supports datadoghq.com, datadoghq.eu, and other Datadog sites
author: Gantry

name: grafana
title: Grafana
description: Embed Grafana dashboard panels directly on Service and Infrastructure entity pages.
longDescription:
The Grafana plugin embeds live dashboard panels from your Grafana instance directly into Gantry entity pages. Set a dashboard UID and panel IDs in entity annotations and Gantry will render the live Grafana visualization inline — no separate browser tab needed.

Works with any self-hosted or Grafana Cloud instance running Grafana 9 or later using a service account token.
features: Embed any Grafana dashboard panel on Service and Infrastructure entity pages
Configure dashboards per-entity via annotations — no code required
Supports Grafana 9+ (self-hosted and Grafana Cloud)
Service account token authentication
Multiple panels per entity page
author: Gantry

grafanaUrl.title: Grafana URL
grafanaUrl.description: Base URL of your Grafana instance

name: argocd
title: ArgoCD
description: Auto-discovers ArgoCD Applications across multiple instances and syncs them to the catalog. Apps sharing the same label value are grouped under one service entity. Correlates with Kubernetes-discovered services to avoid duplicates.
longDescription:
The ArgoCD plugin discovers applications across one or more ArgoCD instances and syncs them to the Gantry service catalog. Each application becomes a Service entity enriched with live sync status, health state, and deployment metadata.

Apps sharing the same label value are grouped under a single Gantry service. The plugin automatically correlates with Kubernetes-discovered entities to avoid duplicates, and fills in the deployment namespace and Git repo URL so the Kubernetes pod panel and GitHub tab activate automatically.
features: Multi-instance ArgoCD discovery and automatic catalog sync
Live sync status, health state, and last-deployed timestamp on entity pages
One-click Sync, Refresh, and Hard Sync from the Gantry UI
Smart deduplication: correlates with Kubernetes plugin to avoid duplicate entities
Populates deployment namespace for automatic Kubernetes pod panel activation
Populates repo URL for automatic GitHub panel activation
Token or username/password authentication per instance
App label grouping: multiple ArgoCD apps map to a single Gantry service
author: Gantry

instances.title: ArgoCD Instances
instances.description: One or more ArgoCD instances to discover applications from.

name.title: Instance Name
name.description: Display name for this instance (e.g. production, staging)

argocdUrl.title: ArgoCD URL
argocdUrl.description: Base URL of your ArgoCD instance (e.g. https://argocd.example.com)

token.title: API Token
token.description: ArgoCD API token. Generate one via: argocd account generate-token --account <name>. Leave empty to use username/password.

username.title: Username
username.description: ArgoCD username (used when token is not set).

password.title: Password
password.description: ArgoCD password (used when token is not set).

insecureSkipTLS.title: Skip TLS Verification
insecureSkipTLS.description: Disable TLS verification for self-signed certificates.

project.title: Project Filter
project.description: Only sync apps in this ArgoCD project. Leave empty for all projects.

labelKey.title: Service Name Label
labelKey.description: ArgoCD app label used as the Gantry service name. Apps sharing the same label value (e.g. name: my-api) are grouped under one service. Defaults to 'name'.

syncInterval.title: Sync Interval
syncInterval.description: How often to automatically sync (e.g. 5m, 1h). Leave empty to sync manually only.

name: flux
title: Flux CD
description: Trigger Flux GitOps reconciliation actions from the Gantry UI.
longDescription:
The Flux CD plugin enables Gantry Actions to trigger Flux GitOps reconciliation via the Flux notification API webhook. Use it to build self-service deploy workflows where developers can request an immediate reconcile of a Flux Kustomization or HelmRelease directly from the Gantry portal — no kubectl access needed.

Define a `flux-reconcile` action on any entity and point it at the Flux API endpoint.
features: Trigger Flux Kustomization and HelmRelease reconciliation from Gantry
Works with the Flux notification API webhook endpoint
flux-reconcile action type for use in Gantry Action definitions
Enables developer self-service for GitOps deploys without kubectl
author: Gantry

name: terraform-cloud
title: Terraform Cloud
description: Trigger Terraform plan and apply runs and view workspace status on Infrastructure entities.
longDescription:
The Terraform Cloud plugin connects Gantry to your Terraform Cloud (or Terraform Enterprise) organization. It adds a workspace status panel to Infrastructure entities so teams can see the latest run outcome without opening Terraform Cloud, and provides `terraform-plan` and `terraform-apply` action types for self-service infrastructure operations.

Map Gantry Infrastructure entities to Terraform workspaces via annotations to activate the panel automatically.
features: Workspace run status and last-apply outcome on Infrastructure entity pages
terraform-plan and terraform-apply Gantry action types
View plan output and apply status inline
Supports Terraform Cloud and Terraform Enterprise
Annotation-driven workspace mapping — no code required
author: Gantry

name: status-monitor
title: Status Monitor
description: Monitor the health of external services and cloud providers your team depends on, with a live dashboard and sidebar page.
longDescription:
The Status Monitor plugin acts as a built-in down detector for the services your platform depends on. It ships with 30+ pre-configured providers — GitHub, Cloudflare, Datadog, Vercel, Stripe, and more — that are checked automatically via their public status-page APIs.

Once installed and enabled, a new Status page appears in the sidebar showing live operational status for every monitored service. A compact dashboard widget highlights any providers currently experiencing issues so your team is aware of upstream problems without leaving Gantry.

You can also add custom providers by entering any Atlassian Statuspage-compatible URL (or any HTTP endpoint) in the plugin configuration.
features: 30+ pre-configured providers with automatic status checking
Live status page in the sidebar with category and status filters
Dashboard widget showing services with active incidents
Custom provider support — add any Atlassian Statuspage URL or HTTP endpoint
Concurrent status checks with 60-second server-side caching
Zero configuration required — works immediately after install and enable
author: Gantry

customProviders.title: Custom Providers
customProviders.description: Add your own status pages to monitor alongside the built-in providers.

name.title: Provider Name
name.description: Display name for this provider (e.g. My Internal API)

statusUrl.title: Status Page URL
statusUrl.description: Atlassian Statuspage base URL or any HTTP(S) endpoint to check

homepageUrl.title: Homepage URL
homepageUrl.description: Link to the provider's main website (optional)
